questions.subject: Foundation Engineering
questions.stem: A footing applies a gross pressure of $200\,\text{kPa}$ at depth $1.5\,\text{m}$ ($\gamma=18\,\text{kN/m}^3$). The net pressure is _____ kPa.

questions.subject: Foundation Engineering
questions.stem: A footing applies a gross pressure of $250\,\text{kPa}$ at depth $2\,\text{m}$ ($\gamma=20\,\text{kN/m}^3$). The net pressure is _____ kPa.

questions.subject: Foundation Engineering
questions.stem: A strip footing rests at $1\,\text{m}$ depth on clay ($c=30\,\text{kPa}$, $\phi=0$, $\gamma=18\,\text{kN/m}^3$). Using Terzaghi ($N_c=5.7,\ N_q=1,\ N_\gamma=0$), the ultimate bearing capacity is _____ kPa.

questions.subject: Foundation Engineering
questions.stem: The group efficiency of a pile group is generally defined as
options: group capacity ÷ sum of individual pile capacities
sum of pile capacities ÷ group capacity
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\eta_g=\dfrac{Q_{group}}{n Q_{single}}$.

**Working.**
Group/Σ individual.

**Answer.** Group ÷ Σ individual

questions.subject: Foundation Engineering
questions.stem: The ultimate bearing capacity is $300\,\text{kPa}$. With a factor of safety of $3$, the net safe bearing capacity (gross basis) is _____ kPa.

questions.subject: Foundation Engineering
questions.stem: The ultimate bearing capacity is $450\,\text{kPa}$. With a factor of safety of $3$, the net safe bearing capacity (gross basis) is _____ kPa.

questions.subject: Foundation Engineering
questions.stem: Immediate (elastic) settlement of a footing on saturated clay is computed using
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Immediate settlement uses $E_u$, $\nu=0.5$ (no volume change).

**Working.**
$E_u$, $\nu=0.5$.

**Answer.** Undrained $E_u$, $\nu=0.5$
